auth-fabric.subtitle: × Austin Research Clinic — Mid-Trial Serious Adverse Event Report

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Ethan logs into Helix's study portal; Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login.
scenes.lede: Ethan Park, Clinical Study Manager at Helix, opens the study portal for HX-2104. Because SAE reporting is a regulatory-grade function and sponsor pharmacovigilance must be defensible, Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login — biometric proof of identity established at the session level, before any adverse-event action. Witness event #1.

beats.head: Ethan hits the Helix study portal login.
beats.lede: Username and password; the session is ready.

beats.head: Maestro intercepts and invokes CLEAR.
beats.lede: Helix-branded step-up — 'Verify your identity for regulatory compliance.'

beats.head: CLEAR session opens on Ethan's phone.
beats.lede: No app download — a one-tap text link.

beats.head: ID + selfie + biometric match.
beats.lede: CLEAR runs the comparison; the result posts back to Maestro in seconds.

beats.head: Authenticated session opens.
beats.lede: Biometric proof bound to the session. Witness event #1 logged.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Ethan submits a Serious Adverse Event report; Maestro serves the SAE webform — bound to the F1 proof.
scenes.lede: Inside his authenticated session, Ethan initiates a mid-trial SAE report for HX-2104. Maestro renders the SAE-report webform inline — pre-filled with the subject ID, study protocol, and the F1 identity binding. No second IDV — the form inherits the session's biometric proof. Witness event #2.

beats.head: Ethan opens 'Report Serious Adverse Event' in the portal.
beats.lede: Subject identifier, event type, and timing.

beats.head: Maestro renders the SAE webform inline.
beats.lede: Pre-filled with subject ID, protocol version (1.2), study arm, and the F1 identity binding.

beats.head: Ethan adds event details and causality assessment.
beats.lede: Event onset date, severity, relationship to study drug, corrective action taken.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens for completeness.
beats.lede: All mandatory fields captured; SAE meets expedited-reporting threshold (24-hour IRB notification).

beats.head: Submit fires — bound to the established biometric proof.
beats.lede: Witness event #2 logged. IRB and sponsor pharmacovigilance are triggered simultaneously.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK LINKS THE TWO EVENTS
scenes.head: Helix's Agreement Desk shows the auth and the SAE report as one continuous record for IRB audit.
scenes.lede: Maestro has been binding the auth event to the SAE-report event in the orchestration layer the whole time — Agreement Desk is the on-screen proxy that makes that invisible work visible. Helix's regulatory team sees the unified record: the CLEAR biometric authentication at session entry, and the SAE submission — linked as a continuous identity thread. One audit trail. One defensible record. Meets 21 CFR 312.64 and ICH-E2A standards.

beats.head: Agreement Desk opens to Ethan's session activity.
beats.lede: The auth event and the SAE-report event sit in a single view.

beats.head: The link is explicit: 'Verified by CLEAR · Ethan Park · CA DL ***7641 · 09:15 EST' → 'SAE Report · Subject HX-2104-047 · Event Onset 2026-04-26 · 09:22 EST'.
beats.lede: Subject, event description, causality, and reporting tier all visible.

beats.head: Iris confirms completeness and urgency.
beats.lede: Expedited reporting required (24-hour IRB notification + 15-day sponsor report).

beats.head: IRB notification queues automatically.
beats.lede: The SAE package, including the identity-authenticated submission, routes to the IRB within minutes.

beats.head: Defensible record.
beats.lede: If the IRB ever audits the SAE-reporting process, Helix has both halves of the chain — identity proof and event submission linked.

intake.subtitle: × Austin Research Clinic — Site Activation Configuration

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SENDER WEBFORM
scenes.head: Ethan configures the site activation for ARC via Helix's internal site-config webform.
scenes.lede: Ethan Park opens Helix's site-activation webform to onboard Austin Research Clinic as a participating site for HX-2104. Entity name, PI information, protocol version, IRB approval date, budget tier, and investigator compensation structure all drive the configuration. Maestro will fan out the activation packet from these selections.

beats.head: Ethan selects 'New Site Activation' from Helix's intake menu.
beats.lede: Sponsor intake — internal webform.

beats.head: He fills entity name, PI name, and medical license number.
beats.lede: Austin Research Clinic, Dr. Karim Halim, CA Medical Board License #186542.

beats.head: He enters protocol version, IRB approval date, and budget tier.
beats.lede: Protocol 1.2, IRB approval 2026-04-15, $150k annual budget tier for first 25 enrollments.

beats.head: He selects the investigator compensation structure.
beats.lede: Milestone-based (per-enrolled-subject + completion bonus). Each selection branches what FD&A disclosure forms attach.

beats.head: Ethan reviews the configured package summary.
beats.lede: 5 documents, 2 recipients (PI + Sponsor Legal), sequenced routing, all pre-populated from site and protocol data.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro fans out the site-activation packet: CTA + Form 1572 + financial disclosure + IB acknowledgment + protocol signature page.
scenes.lede: The moment Ethan submits the webform, Maestro orchestrates: it pulls the CTA template for the protocol, the FDA Form 1572 for the site, the financial-disclosure form for Dr. Halim, the Investigator's Brochure acknowledgment, and the protocol signature page. Each document pre-fills with HX-2104 and ARC site data. The recipient list is built. The envelope is ready.

beats.head: Maestro pulls the right CTA variant.
beats.lede: US domestic site, CRO-managed, protocol 1.2 = a specific CTA version tied to the current protocol amendment.

beats.head: Form FDA 1572 auto-populates with site + PI data.
beats.lede: Site name, IRB location, PI license, institution address — 14 fields from Helix's system of record.

beats.head: Financial-disclosure form attaches with Dr. Halim's compensation tier.
beats.lede: Milestone-based, compensation details pre-filled.

beats.head: IB v5 acknowledgment and protocol signature page attach.
beats.lede: Current protocol version, amendment status, all regulatory cross-references embedded.

beats.head: Recipient routing built: Dr. Halim first, Sponsor Legal second.
beats.lede: CTA + 1572 + FD&A disclosure + IB + protocol page — all ready to send.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Dr. Halim reviews and signs the site-activation packet in a Helix-branded ceremony.
scenes.lede: The packaged envelope arrives at Dr. Halim in seconds. Helix-branded email, signing ceremony with the five docs in order, identity verified at signing time via CLEAR (license check runs in parallel). Sponsor legal review fires automatically upon Dr. Halim's completion.

beats.head: Helix-branded email lands in Dr. Halim's inbox.
beats.lede: No login required — one link to begin.

beats.head: CLEAR step-up initiates for license verification.
beats.lede: State medical board license check runs in parallel with signing.

beats.head: Ceremony opens with the five docs in order.
beats.lede: Yellow START tab guides the PI. CTA, Form 1572, financial disclosure, IB acknowledgment, protocol signature page.

beats.head: Signature applied across the five docs.
beats.lede: One ceremony, five documents, license verified.

beats.head: Multi-recipient routing: Sponsor Legal next.
beats.lede: Dr. Halim's completed packet routes to Helix's legal and regulatory team automatically.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · SITE RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Helix, Dr. Halim, and the IRB continue in one Workspace for the duration of the study.
scenes.lede: Activation closes; the site Workspace opens. Dr. Halim, Helix's study team, and the IRB now have a single shared surface for the engagement going forward — protocol amendments, safety letters, deviation reports, invoicing, and enrollment status in one place. When IB v6 releases, every site sees it simultaneously and the acknowledgment round-robin kicks off from the Workspace.

beats.head: Dr. Halim receives a Workspace invite.
beats.lede: Helix-branded. Access to the site's study information dashboard.

beats.head: Activation summary and study timeline visible to all parties.
beats.lede: Protocol, IRB approval, first-patient-in date, enrollment target, key milestones.

beats.head: Iris tracks the site's progress in the agent rail.
beats.lede: Activity shows every regulatory step completed. Ask Iris answers questions about protocol versions, amendment status, and enrollment timelines.

beats.head: Protocol deviation task open (future).
beats.lede: When Dr. Halim reports a deviation, the task drills into the deviation workflow — description, root cause, corrective action, IRB notification. All tracked, no email chains.

beats.head: Site activated — study in motion.
beats.lede: Every document executed, every credential verified, site and sponsor in the same Workspace. Helix's regulatory team has the complete audit trail from day one through first patient out.

intake.subtitle: × Marcus Chen — Silver HMO Enrollment via Marketplace

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE PORTAL
scenes.head: Marcus completes his Silver HMO enrollment on Unity's member portal.

Marcus — new to the marketplace — fills out the Silver HMO application: household size, income, and tobacco use all drive what fields appear. Plan selection, primary-care physician choice, and payment method configuration are dynamic based on his household profile and subsidy eligibility.

beats.head: Marcus selects 'Enroll in Silver HMO' from the marketplace portal.

beats.lede: No login required — one link to begin.

beats.head: CLEAR step-up initiates for member identity verification.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · MEMBER RELATIONSHIP

Marcus now has a single shared surface for his entire plan experience going forward — ID card, plan documents, prior authorizations, and wellness resources in one place. When formulary updates release, Marcus sees them in the Workspace and can act on changes before his pharmacy encounters them.

auth-fabric.subtitle: × Eric Tan — Revoke or Modify Records Release Scope

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 3 · AUTHENTICATED PORTAL ENTRY · CLEAR AT THE DOOR
scenes.head: Eric logs into Catalina's patient portal; Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login.
scenes.lede: Eric Tan opens Catalina Medical's patient portal. Because release-of-information scope changes are a sensitive privacy control and patient impersonation is a known ROI surface, Maestro orchestrates CLEAR at login — biometric proof of identity established at the session level, before any scope-modification action. Witness event #1.

beats.head: Eric hits Catalina's patient portal login.
beats.lede: Username and password; the session is ready.

beats.head: Maestro intercepts and invokes CLEAR.
beats.lede: Catalina-branded step-up — 'Verify your identity to modify your records release.'

beats.head: ID + selfie + biometric match.

beats.head: Authenticated session opens.
beats.lede: Biometric proof bound to the session. Witness event #1 logged.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 3 · IN-SESSION ACTION · MAESTRO SERVES WEBFORM
scenes.head: Eric modifies his records-release scope; Maestro serves the modification webform — bound to the F1 proof.
scenes.lede: Inside his authenticated session, Eric opens 'Manage My Records Release.' Maestro renders the scope-modification webform inline — pre-filled with his current active releases, recipients, and the F1 identity binding. No second IDV — the form inherits the session's biometric proof. Witness event #2.

beats.head: Eric views his current active records releases.
beats.lede: Recipient (Silver Mountain Disability), scope (18 months of clinical + lab + imaging), and effective dates all listed.

beats.head: Maestro renders the modification webform inline.
beats.lede: Pre-filled with current release details, modification type (revoke/narrow scope), effective date, and the F1 identity binding.

beats.head: Eric selects 'Narrow scope' and removes imaging records.
beats.lede: Remaining scope = clinical notes + lab results only. Effective immediately.

beats.head: Iris pre-screens for validity.
beats.lede: Patient is the MRN holder, modification is within his control, no court order or legal hold present.

beats.head: Submit fires — bound to the established biometric proof.
beats.lede: Witness event #2 logged. Catalina's HIM system is notified; active release to Silver Mountain is updated in real time.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK LINKS THE TWO EVENTS
scenes.head: Catalina's Agreement Desk shows the auth and the scope modification as one continuous record for HIPAA audit.
scenes.lede: Maestro has been binding the auth event to the modification event in the orchestration layer the whole time — Agreement Desk is the on-screen proxy that makes that invisible work visible. Catalina's HIM and compliance teams see the unified record: the CLEAR biometric authentication at session entry, and the scope-modification submission — linked as a continuous identity thread. One audit trail. One defensible record. Patient privacy control is authenticated; release suspension is immediate.

beats.head: Agreement Desk opens to Eric's session activity.
beats.lede: The auth event and the scope-modification event sit in a single view.

beats.head: The link is explicit: 'Verified by CLEAR · Eric Tan · CA ID ***3847 · MRN 487624 · 11:09 EST' → 'Scope Modification · Narrow to Clinical + Lab · Revoke Imaging · 11:14 EST'.
beats.lede: Previous scope, new scope, and effective date all visible and authenticated.

beats.head: Iris confirms patient control and absence of legal hold.
beats.lede: Patient is the MRN holder, no HIPAA Business Associate dispute, no court order blocking modification.

beats.head: Silver Mountain Disability's active release is updated in HIM.
beats.lede: Imaging records are immediately excluded from future releases; clinical + lab scope remains active.

beats.head: Defensible record.
beats.lede: If Catalina is ever audited on this ROI request, HIM has both halves of the chain — patient identity proof and scope modification linked with timestamp.

intake.subtitle: × Eric Tan — HIM Intake from Inbound ROI Request

scenes.head: Renata kicks off the HIM intake for an inbound records-release request.

beats.lede: Witness event #2 logged. Registrar's office is notified; transcript release to Columbia is queued with Priya's identity authenticated.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 3 · CHAIN OF CUSTODY · AGREEMENT DESK LINKS THE TWO EVENTS
scenes.head: Maple Ridge's Registrar sees the auth and the transcript request as one continuous FERPA-defensible record.
scenes.lede: Maestro has been binding the auth event to the transcript-request event in the orchestration layer the whole time — Agreement Desk is the on-screen proxy that makes that invisible work visible. Maple Ridge's registrar and compliance teams see the unified record: the CLEAR biometric authentication at session entry, and the transcript-release submission — linked as a continuous identity thread. One audit trail. One FERPA-defensible record. Student identity is unambiguously proven; transcript release is authenticated at the source.

beats.head: Agreement Desk opens to Priya's session activity.
beats.lede: The auth event and the transcript-request event sit in a single view.

beats.head: The link is explicit: 'Verified by CLEAR · Priya Shah · CA ID ***9271 · Student ID 287461 · 10:42 EST' → 'Transcript Release Request · Columbia University · Philosophy · 10:47 EST'.
beats.lede: Recipient institution, department, and release scope all visible and authenticated.

beats.head: Iris confirms student identity and institutional authorization.
beats.lede: Student is the record holder, Columbia is accredited, FERPA scope compliant.

beats.head: Registrar approves and queues the release.
beats.lede: Official transcript is produced and mailed to Columbia with FERPA audit logging.

beats.head: Defensible record.
beats.lede: If Maple Ridge is ever audited on transcript releases under FERPA, the university has both halves of the chain — student identity proof and request submission linked with timestamp.

intake.subtitle: × Priya Shah — Financial-Aid Acceptance and MPN Signature

scenes.tag: SCENE 1 OF 4 · INTAKE · SELF-SERVICE PORTAL
scenes.head: Priya completes financial-aid acceptance on Maple Ridge's student portal.
scenes.lede: Priya Shah logs into Maple Ridge's student portal during aid-acceptance period. Priya — Fall 2026 admitted student — accepts her financial-aid package and configures her loan and parent-PLUS elections: aid package tier, federal-loan acceptance, parent-PLUS election. Each selection drives what forms appear in the package Maestro will assemble.

beats.head: Priya selects 'Accept Financial Aid Package' from the student portal.
beats.lede: Self-service intake — student fills the webform directly.

beats.head: She reviews her aid award: grants, subsidized loan, unsubsidized loan.
beats.lede: $25k grant, $3.5k subsidized, $2k unsubsidized. Combined with family PLUS borrowing.

beats.head: She accepts the full aid package or configures loan amounts.
beats.lede: Accept all, or decline the unsubsidized portion. Each choice branches which loan forms attach.

beats.head: She elects parent-PLUS if she has eligible parents.
beats.lede: Parent name, relationship, and approval for parent-PLUS application.

beats.head: Priya reviews the configured package summary.
beats.lede: 5 documents, 2 recipients (Priya + parent if parent-PLUS), all pre-populated from her entries and the aid-award data.

scenes.tag: SCENE 2 OF 4 · MAESTRO PACKAGES
scenes.head: Maestro fans out the aid-acceptance packet: award letter + MPN + entrance counseling cert + parent-PLUS app + FERPA disclosure.
scenes.lede: The moment Priya submits the webform, Maestro orchestrates: it pulls the aid award letter, the Master Promissory Note for loans Priya accepted, the entrance-counseling completion certificate, the parent-PLUS application (if elected), and the FERPA disclosure. Each document pre-fills with Priya's aid package, loan selections, and family information. The recipient routing is built. The envelope is ready.

beats.head: Maestro pulls the right financial-aid acceptance variant.
beats.lede: Priya's aid tier, loan selections (subsidized + unsubsidized), and parent-PLUS election = a specific document set.

beats.head: Award letter auto-populates with grant and loan amounts.
beats.lede: Aid package total, disbursement schedule, terms and conditions, cost of attendance calculation.

beats.head: Master Promissory Note attaches for the loans Priya accepted.
beats.lede: Loan type, amount, repayment terms, interest-rate disclosure, life-of-loan terms.

beats.head: Entrance-counseling certificate and parent-PLUS application attach.
beats.lede: Entrance counseling completion proof (required before first loan disbursement) and parent-PLUS app pre-filled with parent info if elected.

beats.head: Recipient routing built: Priya first, parent second if parent-PLUS.
beats.lede: Award + MPN + entrance cert + parent-PLUS + FERPA — all ready to send.

scenes.tag: SCENE 3 OF 4 · ENVELOPE DELIVERED
scenes.head: Priya and parent (if parent-PLUS) sign the financial-aid acceptance in a Maple Ridge-branded ceremony.
scenes.lede: The packaged envelope arrives at Priya in seconds. Maple Ridge-branded email, signing ceremony with the aid documents in order, identity verified at signing time via CLEAR. If parent-PLUS is elected, parent receives a separate signing package and Maestro sequences both signatures before disbursement unlocks.

beats.head: Maple Ridge-branded email lands in Priya's inbox.
beats.lede: No login required — one link to begin.

beats.head: CLEAR step-up initiates for student identity verification.
beats.lede: Student ID cross-reference runs in the same pass.

beats.head: Ceremony opens with the aid documents in order.
beats.lede: Yellow START tab guides the signer. Award letter, MPN, entrance-counseling cert, FERPA disclosure.

beats.head: Priya's signature applied across her documents.
beats.lede: One ceremony, four documents, student ID verified.

beats.head: If parent-PLUS: parent receives parallel signing ceremony.
beats.lede: Parent-PLUS app routes to parent with identity verification; signatures sequence before aid disbursement.

scenes.tag: SCENE 4 OF 4 · WORKSPACE TAIL · STUDENT RELATIONSHIP
scenes.head: Maple Ridge, Priya, and her parent continue in one Workspace for aid and enrollment.
scenes.lede: Aid acceptance closes; the student aid-relationship Workspace opens. Priya and her parent (if parent-PLUS) now have a single shared surface for financial-aid questions and future aid years — loan status, disbursement schedule, repayment roadmap, and future FAFSA prompts in one place. When Priya enters repayment, the Workspace transitions to show repayment options and income-driven plan tools.

beats.head: Priya receives a Workspace invite.
beats.lede: Maple Ridge-branded. Access to her aid summary, loan status, and disbursement schedule.

beats.head: Aid package and tasks visible from day one.
beats.lede: Overview shows grant + loan total, disbursement dates, and loan repayment schedule. Tasks shows any open items — entrance-counseling completion (if not done), parent-PLUS awaiting parent signature, or disbursement confirmations.

beats.head: Iris monitors Priya's aid status in the agent rail.
beats.lede: Activity shows aid acceptance completion. Ask Iris answers questions about loan terms, repayment options, FERPA scope, and future aid eligibility grounded in the signed MPN.

beats.head: FAFSA reminder task (future).
beats.lede: Next academic year, Maple Ridge's financial-aid office opens a task in the Workspace: 'New FAFSA filing deadline approaching.' Priya completes FAFSA from the same Workspace, no new portal login.

beats.head: Aid relationship ongoing.
beats.lede: Financial-aid acceptance executed, parent-PLUS (if any) signed, Priya and Maple Ridge in the same Workspace. The aid relationship runs from here through graduation and into repayment.
